title: $WEBKEY
text: $WEBKEY Specifies a unique string to be used as a key in calculating the hash-values used to protect non-editable fields from being modified in a web browser. Note: Deprecated (although still accepted). Use $SERVER_SECRET in the [WEB] section instead. $WEBKEY { = } Value Defaults Assignment file: wasv.asn Section: [SETTINGS] Default value: Generated during installation Description This setting is an alias for $SERVER_SECRET in the [WEB] section of the assignment file. If both are defined, Uniface will use the last value defined. For more information, see $SERVER_SECRET . CAUTION: The key should be kept secret, so you need to control access to the assignment file of the Web Application Server (by default, wasv.asn ) so that only the Uniface Server ( userver ) process can read it. [SETTINGS] $WEBKEY = JIOhcvjsqj8vsadv;394hwe028*&^ijhe8762l;v93kns2-0gvr;b-05nm2k3;lkcv;flb0-76-u76-0g45nj348h&^Z Copy Related Links Web Application Configuration and Deployment When you deploy and configure the web application you need to ensure that the application is securely deployed. To do so, the passwords and security mechanisms provided with Uniface must be changed and secured. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
